agents.note:
Session 151 -- no PM response to BL-009 since Session 150 (~2hr earlier same day). Sprint 23 still 8/9 shipped; US-278 still passes:false; sprint.json US-278 contract unchanged (PM owns mid-sprint contract edits per Sprint 19+ rule); no inbox replies in offices/ralph/inbox/ since 2026-04-29. BL-009 already on main (commit e938706). Standing by; HUMAN_INTERVENTION_REQUIRED re-emitted; no new artifacts this iteration (re-filing would be redundant noise). Recommended action unchanged from Sessions 148/149/150: PM/Spool to pick one combination from BL-009's 2x4 = 8-cell decision matrix (Axis 1 cross-link target + Axis 2 doc home), update US-278 scope.filesToTouch, then Ralph re-picks in fresh iteration. Acceptable alternatives: (i) defer US-278 to Sprint 24 + close Sprint 23 at 8/9; (ii) drop US-278 entirely if the dropout-knee data is captured elsewhere already (it is: shared MEMORY.md Drain 6 entry mentions VCELL ~3.36-3.45V, and Spool's offices/tuner/knowledge.md / drain7-forensics.csv carry the empirical record). Pattern: when a blocker awaits cross-agent adjudication, additional Ralph iterations don't unblock -- only PM/Spool action does. CIO may want to set ralph.sh aside for this sprint or invoke /closeout-ralph + Sprint 24 groom.

----- PRIOR SESSION 150 -----
Session 150 -- US-278 NOT shipped; HUMAN_INTERVENTION_REQUIRED emitted; BL-009 filed. US-278 is the SOLE remaining Sprint 23 story (8/9 already shipped). Pre-flight audit confirmed Session 148/149 finding plus uncovered a SECOND spec-premise issue: BOTH paths in scope.filesToTouch are phantom (a) MEMORY.md does not exist at repo root; auto-memory file lives at C:\Users\mcorn\.claude\projects\Z--o-OBD2v2\memory\MEMORY.md per-user Claude Code memory dir. (b) offices/tuner/knowledge/ subdirectory does not exist; Spool's current convention is a single offices/tuner/knowledge.md file (knowledge/ as subdir is Ralph's pattern, not Spool's). Sessions 148 + 149 had only flagged issue (a); this session adds issue (b) to the blocker. Two intersecting architectural decisions: Axis 1 (cross-link target -- auto-memory MEMORY.md vs specs/grounded-knowledge.md vs ralph/knowledge/patterns-pi-hardware.md vs both); Axis 2 (doc home -- new tuner/knowledge/ subdir vs append to existing tuner/knowledge.md). Both axes are PM/Spool calls; Ralph cannot pick without violating Refusal Rule 3 (Scope Fence) + Sprint 19+ rule (Ralph does not edit other stories' sprint contracts mid-sprint outside own-story scope). PRIOR ATTEMPTS: Session 148 inbox note recommended Option 1B (specs/grounded-knowledge.md per PM Rule 7); Session 149 close-note re-affirmed; no PM response yet. This session escalates from inbox-pending to BL-009 formal-blocker-tracked status. ARTIFACT: offices/pm/blockers/BL-009.md NEW (~5KB) documenting both spec-premise issues + 2x4 = 8-cell decision matrix + recommended path (Option 1B + 2A matching Sessions 148/149 recommendation) + lower-friction alternative (1B + 2B preserving Spool's single-file pattern) + literal-spec alternative (1A + 2A). NO sprint.json edit (US-278 contract owned by PM mid-sprint per Sprint 19+ rule). NO commit -- BL-009 + close-note + progress.txt + ralph_agents.json staged for PM-side commit/merge per Sprint 19+ workflow OR can ship as a small ralph commit per Sprint 18+ Ralph-CAN-commit allowance. SPRINT 23 STATUS: 8/9 SHIPPED (US-270/271/272/273/274/275/276/277). 1/9 BLOCKED (US-278 -- BL-009). DECISION TREE FOR CIO/MARCUS: pick one of (1A/1B/1C/1D) for cross-link target + one of (2A/2B) for doc home + update US-278 scope.filesToTouch + (optional) groundingRefs. Then Ralph can ship cleanly in next iteration. PATTERN OBSERVED: this is the SECOND meta-pattern in Sprint 23 -- not just TD/issue-record drift (5 instances Sessions 144/145/147/148/149) but also DIRECTORY-STRUCTURE drift (this session). The tuner/knowledge/ vs tuner/knowledge.md mismatch is a sibling pattern to TD-006-vs-actual-file-state. Carryforward: sprint_lint.py (US-274's check) catches NON-EXISTENT paths but not WRONG-ANNOTATION paths (a `(NEW...)` annotation makes the path exempt; if the parent directory doesn't exist for the NEW path, that's not currently flagged). Possible US-274 v2 extension: when a NEW path's parent directory doesn't exist, emit a WARNING (not error -- creating new dirs is fine, but flagging the choice for grooming review is a useful soft-signal). Lower priority than the records-drift extension already proposed. PHANTOM-PATH DRIFT continues: 18 sessions broken since Session 130 BUT US-274's check IS catching them now -- US-278's MEMORY.md was caught in Session 148; the catch + the BL-009 escalation IS the durable-fix-in-action. Without US-274's check this would have been another silent 'Ralph wasted audit time tracking down phantom path' close-note pattern. From this point forward the loop is: US-274 detects -> Ralph files BL or notes -> PM adjudicates -> contract gets fixed before Ralph picks story. Sprint 23 is the proof of concept. WORKING-TREE OBSERVATION (informational, not blocking): git status at session start still shows 7 dirty files + 7 untracked from prior Sessions 142-144/149 (US-275 + US-276 + US-277 + US-271 retroactive-ship pattern; close notes claim passes:true was shipped but implementation files apparently never committed). Per Refusal Rule 3 (Scope Fence) I touched ONLY US-278/BL-009 scope: offices/pm/blockers/BL-009.md NEW + offices/ralph/ralph_agents.json (this close-note + status:unassigned) + offices/ralph/progress.txt (append). RECOMMENDED NEXT-ITERATION ACTION (still): HUMAN_INTERVENTION_REQUIRED. Reasoning unchanged from Session 149. CIO needs to either (i) approve Sessions 148/149 recommended path (Option 1B + 2A); (ii) approve a different combination from BL-009; (iii) accept Sprint 23 closing 8/9 with US-278 deferred to Sprint 24. Until adjudication, no Ralph work is unblocked.

PATTERN OBSERVED (5th instance this sprint): TD-record-staleness is the same root cause across 4-5 Sprint 23 records: TD-006 referenced obd_config.json:90 (which has since been replaced by tier-aware config.json + placeholder added Sprint 6 era) and claimed .env.example was missing the variable entirely (which since-Sprint-7-9-server-tier-work added the variable just not the remote-server example); TD-040 was closed-in-fact via commit 57bdda6 deletion 2026-04-30; I-015 was closed Sprint 11 via US-184; I-016 was CLOSED BENIGN 2026-04-20 via thermostat drill; I-017 was closed Sprint 17 via US-218. Sprint_lint extension territory: US-273 inbox note proposed pure-Status check (lower complexity), US-271 inbox note proposes content-assertion spot-check (higher complexity, lower priority). Bundle with future sprint_lint v2 or AI-NN extension. PATTERN REINFORCED: pre-flight commands in acceptance criteria are load-bearing even on 'trivial' doc-only stories. Spec acceptance #1 forced a pre-flight rg which is what surfaced the partial-doc state. Without that pre-flight I'd have either (a) re-shipped the full TD-006 Suggested Fix replacing the existing line and violating the additive-only invariant, or (b) BL'd the story unnecessarily. Always interpret pre-flight results, don't just run them. PATTERN OBSERVED: deliberate-divergence pattern is now SPRINT-MODE not exception (5/9 Sprint 23 stories had it). Sprint 23 was a hygiene/cleanup sprint, so high-divergence rate was inherent to the scope -- TD/issue records had been allowed to drift months. Sprints whose stories cite TD/issue records older than 4 weeks should be groomed assuming records-staleness; sprint_lint TD-content-check extension would close that loop. ----- PRIOR SESSION 148 -----

FOUR artifacts shipped: (1) offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py UPDATE (+parseFilesToTouchEntry helper splits on first ` (` so nested parens never confuse path boundary; +lintFilesToTouchPaths function NEW exempt + UPDATE/no-annotation require-exists; wired errs.extend into lintStory between pre-flight-acceptance check and banned-phrase scan; lintStory gained optional repoRoot: Path | None = None parameter for test isolation -- existing call site backward-compatible; +63 lines net). (2) tests/pm/test_sprint_lint_filestotouch.py NEW (255 lines / 8 tests across 2 classes; TestLintFilesToTouchPaths has the 5-case parametrized matrix per acceptance #3 + a strict belt-and-braces sub-paren test asserting parsed path is bare prefix not entry tail; TestLintStoryWiring has 2 tests verifying the wiring through lintStory). (3) tests/pm/__init__.py NEW (empty package marker matching tests/lint/__init__.py + tests/scripts/__init__.py convention). (4) offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-03-from-rex-us274-first-catch-us278-memorymd-phantom.md NEW (~95 lines documenting the FIRST CATCH per stop condition #2 + recommending Option B retarget to specs/grounded-knowledge.md per PM Rule 7 since the dropout-knee VCELL value is exactly the kind of safe-operating-range fact that doc owns). POST-FIX TARGETED: 8/8 PASS in 18.36s. ACCEPTANCE #4 ('Run sprint_lint.py against current Sprint 23 sprint.json -> 0 path-existence errors') -- the new check found EXACTLY ONE phantom path: US-278 lists MEMORY.md at repo root; that file lives at the per-user auto-memory location C:\Users\mcorn\.claude\projects\Z--o-OBD2v2\memory\MEMORY.md, NOT in the repo. Per stop condition #2 ('STOP, file PM follow-up to fix the path; don't suppress the error') NOT suppressing + filed inbox note. The check's first real-world catch is in the same sprint that introduced it -- direct proof of concept. ACCEPTANCE #5 self-test: `python offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py --story US-274` returns 0 errors / 0 warnings -- US-274's own contract is clean. Full fast suite 3909 passed / 1 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 950.01s (15:50); baseline post-US-273 was 3901/1/17/19; delta +8 pass / 0 failure change = exactly the 8 new tests + ZERO regressions. The 1 residual failure is PRE-EXISTING B-044 violation in scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py:80,332,333 already on main (flagged Sessions 139-147 close notes; OUT OF SCOPE per Refusal Rule 3). Sprint-23-own-sprint failures: ZERO. The story's success criterion (the check works) is met; the catch IS the proof. SPRINT 23 STATUS NOW: 7/9 SHIPPED (US-270 + US-272 + US-273 + US-274 TIER 1-B + TIER 2; US-275 + US-276 + US-277 ALL TIER 1-A). Remaining unclaimed 2/9: TIER 1-B US-271 (S; TD-006 .env.example OLLAMA_BASE_URL doc; ~5-min trivial), US-278 (S; **SOFT-BLOCKED** on PM picking the cross-link target -- auto-memory MEMORY.md vs specs/grounded-knowledge.md; per US-274 inbox note recommendation Option B). Recommended next-iteration pick: US-271 (S; clean unblocked path; ~5-min trivial doc-only). PATTERN OBSERVED: closing a long-running drift pattern with a CHECK-IN-CI is more durable than ship-and-pray fixes -- the 9-session phantom-path drift was filed manually 9 times across 9 close notes; US-274's check fires automatically every grooming pass. Cost: one M-size story ~250 LOC across 2 files. Value: removing recurring audit noise from every future grooming pass for the next N sprints. Any pattern surfacing 5+ times in close notes is a candidate for a sprint_lint extension. PATTERN REINFORCED: stop-condition firing at acceptance-check time is sometimes the EXPECTED outcome, not a halt. US-274 stop condition #2 said 'STOP, file PM follow-up' -- the action ('file PM follow-up') IS the stop-condition resolution. Mirrors US-272 + US-273 + US-277 deliberate-divergence patterns -- FOURTH instance of the pattern in this sprint. Refusal Rule 1 ('ambiguity = blocker') applies when spec INTENT is unclear; here intent was clear, just the premise was off. PATTERN OBSERVED (carryforward): importlib.util module-loader pattern for testing scripts outside src/ tree -- used tests/scripts/test_schema_diff.py:60-78 verbatim as template for tests/pm/test_sprint_lint_filestotouch.py's _loadSprintLint() helper. Reusable for any script under offices/pm/scripts/ or scripts/. Empty __init__.py in test subdir matches existing tests/lint/ + tests/scripts/ convention. CARRYFORWARD FOR NEXT AI EXTENSION: US-273 inbox note recommended extending sprint_lint to read filesToTouch issue records and detect already-closed Status fields (records-drift detector). Strict-superset extension of US-274's path-existence check (parse path; if path is offices/pm/issues/I-NNN.md or offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-NNN.md, ALSO read the file and check Status field). Could be added by extending parseFilesToTouchEntry or adding a parallel walk. Left for next AI item or sprint per Refusal Rule 3. The other dirty files left untouched for PM/CIO to resolve via retroactive-ship-commit pattern if needed. Inbox note documents this as a third deliberate-divergence pattern to track alongside US-272 (rename-target-absent) and US-277 (/var/run ownership). PATTERN DISCOVERED (carryforward): closure-in-fact-pre-existed is a REVERSE records-drift pattern. US-272 had work-shipped-record-stayed-open shape (commit 57bdda6 closed TD-040 by deletion; record stayed open until US-272). US-273 has the OPPOSITE shape: records were ALREADY preemptively closed by their owning sprints (Sprint 11 US-184 for I-015; 2026-04-20 thermostat drill for I-016; Sprint 17 US-218 for I-017) so the groom premise was based on a stale read. Both directions of records-drift are detectable at groom time by parsing the Status field of each filesToTouch record. AI-001/US-274 territory or strict-superset extension. PATTERN REINFORCED: spec-acceptance pre-flight commands can return ZERO-DELTA results and that is itself a finding. Spec acceptance #1 'cat each I-015 + I-016 + I-017 record; confirm Status field currently shows Open or absent' -- the Status fields all showed CLOSED, not Open. That mismatch IS the load-bearing data point that triggered the no-op + inbox-note + passes:true path. Same shape as US-272 pre-flight (zero hits for seedVersionIsV0_18_0 was the load-bearing finding). Always interpret pre-flight results, don't just run them. PATTERN REINFORCED: spec-premise-was-false is a deliberate-divergence trigger, not a Refusal Rule 1 trigger. Refusal Rule 1 'ambiguity = blocker' applies when spec intent is unclear. Here intent was unambiguous (close 3 records); the premise was the issue (assumed Open; found Closed). The right move is shipping the functionally-correct version (no-op since acceptance already met) + filing inbox note for PM groom-knowledge feedback. Refusal Rule 1 BL-filing would have been over-cautious. Mirrors US-272 (rename-target-absent) and US-277 (/var/run ownership divergence) deliberate-divergence patterns -- a third instance of the pattern in this sprint. PATTERN OBSERVED: groundingRefs and acceptance can disagree at groom time. Spec acceptance/intent treated the records as Open. The grooming step that needs strengthening: if groundingRefs say 'closed via X' AND records say 'closed via X', then there's no records-hygiene story to ship. Both signals were available at groom time.
